prompt:
Platform ticket: build `rs-taskdeps`, the deterministic artifact-graph task planner/runner we keep re-implementing badly inside every service's release tooling. The crate is checked in with its full acceptance suite (tests/plan.rs, tests/execute.rs) but src/ does not exist yet, so `cargo test --offline` currently fails to compile — implement the library until the whole suite is green. Rust 2021, stdlib only (no external crates). Do not touch tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore.

Model: a pipeline is a flat list of task specs, `TaskSpec::new(id: &str, needs: &[&str], produces: &[&str])`. Tasks are connected only through named artifacts: a task is runnable once every artifact it needs exists. Artifacts listed in `given` exist before anything runs. Task ids and artifact names are separate namespaces.

Public API (tests import `rs_taskdeps::{execute, plan, render_plan, PlanError, RunReport, TaskSpec, TaskStatus}`):

1. `pub fn plan(tasks: &[TaskSpec], given: &[&str]) -> Result<Vec<Vec<String>>, PlanError>` — topological batches: batch 1 is every task runnable from `given` alone, batch 2 everything runnable once batch 1's artifacts exist, and so on. Task ids within a batch are sorted lexicographically. An empty task list plans to no batches. Validation runs in this exact order, first failure wins:
   - `DuplicateTask { id }` — an id declared twice (scan declaration order, report the first repeat). Display: `duplicate task id 'lint'`.
   - `DuplicateProducer { artifact, producers }` — an artifact with more than one producer; `given` counts as a producer under the pseudo-name `(given)`. If several artifacts conflict, report the lexicographically smallest artifact; `producers` is sorted lexicographically. Display: `artifact 'binary' has multiple producers: a, b`.
   - `UnknownArtifact { task, artifact }` — a needed artifact that no task produces and `given` doesn't cover. Scan tasks in lexicographic id order and each task's needs in declaration order; report the first hit. Display: `task 'deploy' needs unknown artifact 'image'`.
   - `Cycle { tasks }` — when scheduling stalls, list every still-unscheduled task (cycle members plus anything wedged behind them), sorted. Display: `dependency cycle among tasks: a, b`.
   `PlanError` needs Debug + PartialEq and the Display strings above, verbatim.

2. `pub fn render_plan(batches: &[Vec<String>]) -> String` — dry-run rendering: one line per batch, `batch 1: a, b` (1-based, ids joined with a comma-space), lines joined with `\n`, no trailing newline, empty plan renders the empty string.

3. `pub fn execute(tasks: &[TaskSpec], given: &[&str], run: &mut dyn FnMut(&str) -> bool) -> Result<RunReport, PlanError>` — plans first (same errors; on a plan error `run` must never be invoked), then walks the batches in order, invoking `run(task_id)` for each runnable task (`true` = success, `false` = failure). A task whose needed artifacts all exist runs; a task depending on any failed or skipped producer is NOT invoked and is recorded `Skipped { because }`, where `because` is the id of the root-cause failed task — skips inherit their producer's root cause transitively, and when several roots apply the lexicographically smallest wins. Failed and skipped tasks produce none of their artifacts. `RunReport { pub invoked: Vec<String>, pub statuses: Vec<(String, TaskStatus)> }` — `invoked` in actual invocation order; `statuses` has one entry per task, sorted by id. `TaskStatus { Succeeded, Failed, Skipped { because: String } }` with Debug + Clone + PartialEq.

Determinism is the entire point of extracting this library: no hash-map iteration order may ever leak into batches, error payloads, reports, or rendered plans. Acceptance: `cargo test --offline` fully green (29 tests across the two files).
